title: GraphQL in React Native Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of GraphQL queries, mutations, Apollo Client setup and caching.

questions.question: What is the main mobile advantage of GraphQL over REST for data fetching?

options.text: GraphQL is faster because it uses UDP instead of TCP for transport
The client specifies exactly which fields it needs, eliminating over-fetching and allowing multiple resources to be fetched in one request
GraphQL works offline without a backend server
GraphQL automatically caches all responses on the device for 24 hours

questions.question: What does the Apollo Client's `setContext` link do when configured with `authLink`?

options.text: It sets the React context so all components can access Apollo data
It intercepts every outgoing request and attaches the auth token from AsyncStorage to the Authorization header
It updates the React context with fresh data after every GraphQL response
It validates GraphQL queries against the schema before sending them
It only uses the cache and never makes a network request
It always bypasses the cache and fetches fresh data from the network
It returns cached data immediately to render the UI, then makes a background network request to update the cache with fresh data
It uses the cached data if the device is offline and network data if online

questions.question: What is an optimistic response in Apollo Client mutations?

options.text: A response that only resolves on success and never triggers error handlers
An immediate update to the local cache before the server responds, making the UI feel instant, with automatic rollback if the mutation fails
A mutation that assumes the server will always return 200 OK
A query that fetches multiple resources simultaneously to optimize performance

questions.question: Where should you define your GraphQL queries (gql`...`) in a React Native project?

options.text: In a single centralized queries.ts file for easy maintenance
Co-located with the component that uses them — this makes it clear what data each component needs and aids code maintainability
In the App.tsx file at application root so they are pre-parsed at startup
In a separate backend-side file that is shared with the server
